latest.title: Mountains don't erode the way we think
latest.script_key: mountains don t erode the way we think those smooth slopes they re fake the real action is in the cracks watch how water carves them wider than we ever noticed that s why the tallest peaks stay sharp instead of rounding off did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Cells copy instructions before splitting
latest.script_key: cells have to copy themselves before dividing watch the cell boundary because division only works after the internal instructions are copied and organized then the cell can split the material into two working lives at this scale reproduction is careful logistics which tiny process next

latest.title: Monkeys wash food when grit gets annoying
latest.script_key: monkeys can change habits to solve small problems watch the hands near the food because some monkeys learn useful routines from their group washing or handling food differently can remove grit and make eating easier the habit spreads because it works which primate trick should come next

latest.title: Magnets make invisible fields visible
latest.script_key: magnets can show a hidden force map watch the filings line up because each tiny piece becomes a small magnet in the field together they trace the direction of the force around the magnet the pattern is physics drawing itself which force next

latest.title: Elephants cool blood through giant ears
latest.script_key: elephants use their ears as living radiators watch the ear surface because warm blood flows through those broad veins moving air can dump heat before it spreads through the body that is why the ears are more than decoration in hot places which giant animal trick next

latest.title: Rock layers store ancient environments in stripes
latest.script_key: rock layers are time stamps made of stone watch the stripe pattern because each layer can mark a different setting river mud ocean floor windblown sand or volcanic ash stack enough layers and the cliff becomes a timeline which rock clue should we read next

latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Geysers make land faster than lava
latest.script_key: land grows in a week right here watch that plume it s not smoke it s superheated water blasting up because magma below boils it that s why this field keeps rising like a tiny volcano did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Llamas survive these mountains better than we do
latest.script_key: these llamas can t shiver so they run a different heater inside their feet watch how their toes get pink with blood right before they dig in that s why they can stand on ice like it s a hot sidewalk so if you ever hike up there

latest.title: Desert mountains fool your brain
latest.script_key: these mountains aren t solid rock see how the layers tilt different ways that s not erosion that s the land bending like hot taffy under its own weight the slowest force on earth isn t wind or water it s gravity pulling the crust down that s why valleys open where you least expect

latest.title: desert mountains erode faster than you think
latest.script_key: these peaks disappear fast notice the jagged ridges that s because water doesn t just flow downhill it carves like a sculptor a single storm can strip away inches in hours that s why some desert ranges look like they re melting would you guess this whole mountain used to be twice as tall

latest.title: Lightning makes air explode into thunder
latest.script_key: lightning turns air into a shock wave watch the flash first because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast that air expands suddenly and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder the sound is the atmosphere snapping back which storm signal next

latest.title: Coral reefs are cities built by tiny animals
latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: Bats aren't blind but their face decorations tell a real sto
latest.script_key: watch this one twice notice those leaf like nose flaps that s not decoration those flaps focus the sound bats use to hunt that s why bats with smoother faces often miss prey did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Bats hunt crickets with sound not sight
latest.script_key: bats see with their ears watch this cricket s wings blur but the bat still nails it that s echolocation sound bounces off prey faster than light that s why the bat never misses in the dark did you think bats were blind

latest.title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next
